---
title: "Visualizing Global Seismic Activity"
author: "Cemre Yilmaz"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Visualizing Global Seismic Activity}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
editor_options: 
  markdown: 
    wrap: 72
---

## Introduction

The earthquake.analysis package provides a suite of tools for processing
and visualizing historical earthquake data from the NOAA National
Geophysical Data Center. This vignette demonstrates a typical data
analysis workflow: cleaning raw data, visualizing timelines, and
creating interactive maps.

``` r
library(earthquake.analysis) 
library(dplyr) 
library(ggplot2) 
library(leaflet) 
```

## Data Preprocessing

The package includes functions to transform raw NOAA data into a tidy
format. We handle date conversions and clean location strings to
separate country names from specific regions.

``` r
# Load the raw dataset 
library(earthquake.analysis) 
data(earthquakes)

# Apply the cleaning pipeline
cleaned_data <- earthquakes %>% 
  eq_clean_data()%>% 
  eq_location_clean()
head(cleaned_data %>% 
       select(Date, Country, Location.Name, Mag)) 
```

## Visualizing Earthquake

Timelines To understand the frequency and severity of earthquakes over
time, we use geom_timeline. This tool allows us to compare multiple
countries and visualize the magnitude (point size) and impact (color) of
each event.

Code snippet

``` r
# Filtering for specific Mediterranean countries 
viz_data <- cleaned_data %>% 
  filter(Country %in% c("TURKEY", "GREECE", "ITALY") & Date > as.Date("2000-01-01"))

# Generating the timeline
geom_timeline_label(viz_data, n_max = 7.0) 
```

## Interactive Spatial Mapping

For a more granular view, the package leverages leaflet to create
interactive maps. We can generate custom HTML labels to display key
metrics (Magnitude, Deaths, Location) upon clicking an epicenter.

``` r
# Prepare labels and filter for high-magnitude events 
map_data <- cleaned_data %>% 
  filter(Country == "TURKEY" & Date > as.Date("1990-01-01")) %>% 
  mutate(popup_text = eq_create_label(.))

# Generate interactive map
eq_map(map_data, annot_col = "popup_text") 
```

## Conclusion

The earthquake.analysis package streamlines the path from raw seismic
records to publication-quality visualizations and interactive tools. By
combining statistical rigor with spatial context, it provides deep
insights into historical earthquake patterns.
